steps.title: Identify the reversal signaled by 'seemingly'
steps.content: The eruption seems inhospitable to life, but Boyd's quote will show it's actually “a cradle for biodiversity”—opposite claims. The word 'seemingly' flags that appearance contradicts reality.

steps.title: Apply the concessive transition
steps.content: ”Still” means “despite the earlier impression, here's what's actually true”—bridging the eruption's apparent hostility and the geyser's real biodiversity. Thus (consequence—wrong direction), Specifically (narrowing—not applicable), and In other words (equivalence—but these are opposite) all miss this shift. That matches choice B — B is correct.
